things.name: French Quarter
things.description: The soul of New Orleans — wrought iron balconies, jazz clubs, voodoo shops and the raucous Bourbon Street

things.name: Preservation Hall
things.description: The most authentic traditional jazz venue in the world — intimate concerts every night since 1961

things.name: Garden District Walking Tour
things.description: Antebellum mansions draped in Spanish moss — take the historic St Charles Avenue streetcar

things.name: Commander's Palace
things.description: New Orleans' most celebrated restaurant (since 1893) — the Saturday jazz brunch with 25-cent martinis is legendary

things.name: Café Du Monde
things.description: Open since 1862 — beignets (deep-fried doughnuts buried in powdered sugar) and chicory café au lait

things.name: National WWII Museum
things.description: America's most visited history museum — the definitive telling of the Second World War with original aircraft

things.name: Swamp Tour
things.description: Airboat into the Louisiana bayou — alligators, blue herons, cypress forests draped in Spanish moss

things.name: St Louis Cemetery No. 1
things.description: New Orleans' oldest cemetery (1789) with above-ground tombs — the final resting place of voodoo queen Marie Laveau

things.name: Frenchmen Street Live Music
things.description: The authentic alternative to Bourbon Street — a dozen clubs in one block with jazz, blues and zydeco nightly

things.name: Mardi Gras World
things.description: Year-round museum and workshop where Mardi Gras floats are designed and built — try on costumes
